analysis.reasoning: Core message: The Russian MFA spokesperson states Russia has no plans to send troops to Afghanistan, emphasizing instead expanded cooperation against terrorism and drug trafficking via military-technical collaboration and training specialists. Evaluation: RU1 (West immoral/hostile): Not mentioned; no critique of the West or its allies. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system): Partially related to non-Western cooperation, but the tweet does not frame it as an alternative system or multilateral order; it’s a narrow, functional cooperation statement—insufficient for RU2. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner): Yes—positions Russia as a responsible, non-interventionist actor helping fight terrorism and drug trafficking and training specialists, aligning with benevolent partnership. RU4 (Russian history admirable): Not relevant. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront): No boasting of superiority or achievements. RU6 (Culture/nature/heritage): Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes RU3.
